title: Consistency Training Helps Stop Sycophancy and Jailbreaks
authors: A. Irpan; Alexander Matt Turner; Mark Kurzeja; David K. Elson; Rohin Shah

position.notes: model adopts user beliefs via leading questions; both verifiable factual and subjective prompts implicated

Model capability preservation metrics
impact_card.population: Simulated users via prompt manipulation; no human participants. Evaluated on Gemini 2.5 Flash.
proposed_mitigations: Bias-augmented Consistency Training (BCT): enforces output invariance across prompt augmentations
Activation Consistency Training (ACT): enforces internal activation invariance across prompt augmentations
impact_card.mitigation_outcomes: Both BCT and ACT equally reduce sycophancy on Gemini 2.5 Flash; BCT outperforms ACT on jailbreak reduction. Both preserve model capabilities by using self-generated training data.
impact_card.notes: Paper frames sycophancy and jailbreaking as consistency problems rather than optimal-response problems. Self-supervised paradigm avoids stale data issues. Covers both sycophancy and jailbreak resistance jointly.
